canto_title: The Celestial Procession of the Empyrean

scenes.summary: Beatrice finishes her words and walks along the riverbank, with Dante following closely behind her.
A brilliant light and a sweet melody appear, causing Dante to reflect on the intoxicating nature of Eve's temptation.
Dante experiences celestial joy and calls upon the Muses for assistance in composing his verses.
Seven golden trees appear like candelabra, topped with a light brighter than the full moon.
Dante looks at Virgil in astonishment, prompting Beatrice to scold him for not looking at what follows the light.
A group of people dressed in brilliant white approaches the riverbank.
As Dante moves to better see the procession, he observes colorful trails of light left by the moving flames.
Twenty-four elders, crowned in bluebells, appear under a divided sky singing praises.
Four winged animals with eyes upon their feathers approach the elders.
Dante struggles to describe the creatures, instead directing the reader to the prophecies of Ezekiel.
A triumphal chariot pulled by a griffin moves through the air with majestic wings.
Dante compares the beauty of this chariot to the greatest triumphs of Rome, noting it surpasses them all.
Three women, representing different colors, dance around the right wheel of the chariot.
Four figures dressed in purple celebrate around the left wheel.
Two elderly men appear, one representing the healing arts and the other representing a contrasting, sharper power.
A group of humble figures is followed by a single, wise-looking elderly man.
The elders are described as wearing crowns of red flowers rather than lilies.
A loud thunderclap sounds, causing the procession to halt abruptly before Dante.
